mn49:1.2: that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
mn49:1.5: “Yes, lord,” the monks responded to him.
mn49:2.1: “On one occasion recently I was staying in Ukkaṭṭha in the Subhaga forest at the root of a royal Sal tree.
mn49:2.2: Now on that occasion an evil viewpoint had arisen to Baka Brahmā:
mn49:2.3: ‘This is constant. This is permanent. This is eternal. This is total. This is not subject to falling away—for this does not take birth, does not age, does not die, does not fall away, does not reappear. And there is no other, higher escape.’
mn49:3.1: “So I—having known with my awareness the line of thinking in Baka Brahmā’s awareness—
mn49:3.2: as a strong man would extend his flexed arm or flex his extended arm, vanished into the root of the royal Sal tree in the Subhaga forest in Ukkaṭṭha and appeared in that Brahmā world.
mn49:3.3: Baka Brahmā saw me coming in the distance and,
mn49:3.5: ‘Come, good sir. You are well-come, good sir.
mn49:3.6: It has been long, good sir, since you arranged to come here—
mn49:3.7: for this, good sir, is constant. This is permanent. This is eternal. This is total. This is not subject to falling away—for here one does not take birth, does not age, does not die, does not fall away, does not reappear. And there is no other, higher escape.’
mn49:4.1: “When this was said, I told Baka Brahmā,
mn49:4.2: ‘How immersed in ignorance is Baka Brahmā! How immersed in ignorance is Baka Brahmā! —
mn49:4.3: in that what is actually inconstant he calls “constant.” What is actually impermanent he calls “permanent.” What is actually non-eternal he calls “eternal.” What is actually partial he calls “total.” What is actually subject to falling away he calls “not subject to falling away.”
mn49:4.4: Where one takes birth, ages, dies, falls away, and reappears, he says,
mn49:4.5: “For here one does not take birth, does not age, does not die, does not fall away, does not reappear.”
mn49:4.6: And there being another, higher escape, he says, “There is no other, higher escape.”’
mn49:5.1: “Then Māra, the Evil One, taking possession of an attendant of the Brahmā assembly, said to me,
mn49:5.2: ‘Monk! Monk! Don’t attack him! Don’t attack him! For this Brahmā, monk, is the Great Brahmā, the Conqueror, the Unconquered, the All-Seeing, All-Powerful, the Sovereign Lord, the Maker, Creator, Chief, Appointer and Ruler, Father of All That Have Been and Shall Be.
mn49:5.3: There were, monk, before your time, contemplatives & brahmans in the world who found fault with earth and were disgusted with earth, who found fault with liquid and were disgusted with liquid, who found fault with fire and were disgusted with fire, who found fault with wind and were disgusted with wind, who found fault with beings and were disgusted with beings, who found fault with devas and were disgusted with devas, who found fault with Pajāpati and were disgusted with Pajāpati, who found fault with Brahmā and were disgusted with Brahmā.
mn49:5.4: “‘They, with the break-up of the body, with the cutting off of life, were established in a coarse body.
mn49:5.5: There were, monk, before your time, contemplatives & brahmans in the world who praised earth and were delighted with earth, who praised liquid and were delighted with liquid, who praised fire and were delighted with fire, who praised wind and were delighted with wind, who praised beings and were delighted with beings, who praised devas and were delighted with devas, who praised Pajāpati and were delighted with Pajāpati, who praised Brahmā and were delighted with Brahmā.
mn49:5.6: “‘They, with the break-up of the body, with the cutting off of life, were established in a refined body.
mn49:5.8: “Please, good sir, do only as Brahmā says. Don’t defy the word of Brahmā.
mn49:5.9: If you defy the word of Brahmā, then—as a man, when the goddess of fortune approaches, chases her away with a stick, or as a man, falling into hell, loses hold of the earth with his hands and feet—that will be what you have accomplished.
mn49:5.10: Please, good sir, do only as Brahmā says. Don’t defy the word of Brahmā.
mn49:5.11: Don’t you see that Brahmā’s assembly has gathered?”’
mn49:5.12: And so Māra the Evil One directed my attention to Brahmā’s assembly.
mn49:6.1: “When this was said, I told Māra the Evil One,
mn49:6.2: ‘I know you, Evil One. Don’t assume,
mn49:6.5: And Brahmā, and Brahmā’s assembly, and the attendants of Brahmā’s assembly have all come into your hands. They have all come under your sway.
mn49:6.7: “This one, too, has come into my hands, has come under my sway.”
mn49:6.8: But, Evil One, I have neither come into your hands nor have I come under your sway.’
mn49:7.1: “When this was said, Baka Brahmā told me,
mn49:7.2: ‘But, good sir, what is actually constant I call “constant.” What is actually permanent I call “permanent.” What is actually eternal I call “eternal.” What is actually total I call “total.” What is actually not subject to falling away I call “not subject to falling away.” Where one does not take birth, age, die, fall away, or reappear, I say,
mn49:7.3: “For this does not take birth, does not age, does not die, does not fall away, does not reappear.”
mn49:7.4: And there being no other, higher escape, I say, “There is no other, higher escape.”
mn49:7.5: “‘There were, monk, before your time, contemplatives & brahmans in the world whose ascetic practice lasted as long as your entire life span.
mn49:7.6: They knew, when there was another, higher escape, that there was another, higher escape; or, when there was no other, higher escape, that there was no other, higher escape.
mn49:7.8: both that you will not find another, higher escape, and that, to that extent, you will reap your share of trouble & weariness.
mn49:7.9: Monk, if you relish earth, you will lie close to me, lie within my domain, for me to banish and to do with as I like.
mn49:7.16: Brahmā, you will lie close to me, lie within my domain, for me to banish and to do with as I like.’
mn49:8.2: If I relish earth, I will lie close to you, lie within your domain, for you to banish and to do with as you like.
mn49:8.9: Brahmā, I will lie close to you, lie within your domain, for you to banish and to do with as you like. Moreover, I discern your sphere, I discern your splendor:
mn49:8.10: “Baka Brahmā has this much great power. Baka Brahmā has this much great might. Baka Brahmā has this much great influence.”’
mn49:8.11: “‘Well, monk, how do you discern my sphere, how do you discern my splendor:
mn49:8.12: “Baka Brahmā has this much great power. Baka Brahmā has this much great might. Baka Brahmā has this much great influence”?’
mn49:9.5: There you know those above & below,
mn49:9.7: the state of what is as it is,
mn49:9.8: the state of what becomes otherwise, the coming & going of beings.
mn49:9.9: “‘That, Brahmā, is how I discern your sphere, that is how I discern your splendor:
mn49:9.10: “Baka Brahmā has this much great power. Baka Brahmā has this much great might. Baka Brahmā has this much great influence.”
mn49:10.1: There are, Brahmā, bodies other than yours that you don’t know, don’t see,
mn49:10.2: but that I know, I see.
mn49:10.3: There is, Brahmā, the body named Ābhassarā [Radiant] from which you fell away & reappeared here.
mn49:10.4: From your having lived here so long, your memory of that has become muddled. That is why you don’t know it, don’t see it,
mn49:10.5: but I know it, I see it.
mn49:10.6: Thus I am not your mere equal in terms of direct knowing, so how could I be inferior?
mn49:10.7: I am actually superior to you.
mn49:10.8: “‘There is, Brahmā, the body named Subhakiṇhā [Beautiful Black]… the body named Vehapphalā [Sky-fruit], {the body named Abhibhū [Conqueror]} which you don’t know, don’t see,
mn49:10.9: but that I know, I see.
mn49:10.10: Thus I am not your mere equal in terms of direct knowing, so how could I be your inferior?
mn49:10.11: I am actually superior to you.
mn49:11.1: “‘Having directly known earth as earth, and having directly known the extent of what has not been experienced through the earthness of earth, I wasn’t earth, I wasn’t in earth, I wasn’t coming from earth, I wasn’t “Earth is mine.” I didn’t affirm earth.
mn49:11.2: Thus I am not your mere equal in terms of direct knowing, so how could I be inferior?
mn49:11.3: I am actually superior to you.
mn49:20.1: the beautiful black as the beautiful black…
mn49:23.1: “‘Having directly known the all as the all, and having directly known the extent of what has not been experienced through the allness of the all, I wasn’t the all, I wasn’t in the all, I wasn’t coming forth from the all, I wasn’t “The all is mine.” I didn’t affirm the all.
mn49:23.2: Thus I am not your mere equal in terms of direct knowing, so how could I be inferior?
mn49:23.3: I am actually superior to you.’
mn49:24.1: “‘If, good sir, you have directly known the extent of what has not been experienced through the allness of the all, may it not turn out to be actually vain and void for you.’
mn49:25.3: has not been experienced through the earthness of earth… the liquidity of liquid… the fieriness of fire… the windiness of wind… the allness of the all.’
mn49:26.1: “‘Well then, good sir, I will disappear from you.’
mn49:26.2: “‘Well then, Brahmā, disappear from me if you can.’
mn49:26.4: ‘I will disappear from Gotama the contemplative. I will disappear from Gotama the contemplative,’ was not able to disappear from me.
mn49:26.5: When this was said, I said to Baka Brahmā,
mn49:26.6: ‘Well then, Brahmā, I will disappear from you.’
mn49:26.7: “‘Well then, good sir, disappear from me if you can.’
mn49:26.8: “So then, monks, I fabricated a fabrication of psychic power to the extent that
mn49:26.9: Brahmā, the Brahmā assembly, and the attendants of the Brahmā assembly heard my voice but did not see me.
mn49:26.10: Having disappeared, I recited this verse:
mn49:27.2: in becoming, and becoming in searching for non-becoming,
mn49:27.3: I didn’t affirm any kind of becoming,
mn49:28.1: “Then in Brahmā, the Brahmā assembly, and the attendants of the Brahmā assembly there arose a sense of amazement & astonishment:
mn49:28.3: The great power, the great might of Gotama the contemplative! Never before have we seen or heard of any other contemplative or brahman of such great power, such great might as that of this Gotama the contemplative, who went forth from a Sakyan clan!
mn49:28.4: Living in a generation that so delights in becoming, so rejoices in becoming, is so fond of becoming, he has pulled out becoming by the root!’
mn49:29.1: “Then Māra, the Evil One, taking possession of an attendant of the Brahmā assembly, said to me,
mn49:29.2: ‘If, good sir, this is what you discern, if this is what you have awakened to, do not lead (lay) disciples or those gone forth.
mn49:29.3: Do not teach the Dhamma to (lay) disciples or those gone forth.
mn49:29.4: Do not yearn for (lay) disciples or those gone forth.
mn49:29.5: There were, good sir, before your time, contemplatives & brahmans in the world who claimed to be worthy & rightly self-awakened.
mn49:29.6: They led (lay) disciples & those gone forth. They taught the Dhamma to (lay) disciples & those gone forth. They yearned for (lay) disciples & those gone forth. Having led (lay) disciples & those gone forth, having taught the Dhamma to (lay) disciples & those gone forth, having yearned for (lay) disciples & those gone forth,
mn49:29.7: they—on the break-up of the body, with the cutting off of life—were established in a coarse body.
mn49:29.8: “‘There were, good sir, before your time, contemplatives & brahmans in the world who claimed to be worthy & rightly self-awakened.
mn49:29.9: They did not lead (lay) disciples or those gone forth. They did not teach the Dhamma to (lay) disciples or those gone forth. They did not yearn for (lay) disciples or those gone forth. Having not led (lay) disciples or those gone forth, having not taught the Dhamma to (lay) disciples or those gone forth, having not yearned for (lay) disciples or those gone forth,
mn49:29.10: they—on the break-up of the body, with the cutting off of life—were established in a refined body.
mn49:29.12: Please, good sir, be effortless. Abide committed to a pleasant abiding in the here & now—for it’s skillful, good sir, that this not be taught. Don’t instruct others.’
mn49:30.1: “When this was said, I told Māra the Evil One,
mn49:30.2: ‘I know you, Evil One. Don’t assume,
mn49:30.5: And it’s not sympathetic to welfare that you speak thus to me.
mn49:30.6: It’s sympathetic to what is not welfare that you speak thus to me.
mn49:30.8: “Those to whom Gotama the contemplative will teach the Dhamma will defy my sovereignty”.
mn49:30.9: Without being rightly self-awakened, Evil One, your contemplatives & brahmans claimed to be rightly self-awakened.
mn49:30.10: I, however, being rightly self-awakened, claim to be rightly self-awakened.
mn49:30.11: For when the Tathāgata is teaching the Dhamma to his disciples, he is Such. When he is not teaching the Dhamma to his disciples, he is Such.
mn49:30.12: When leading his disciples he is Such. When not leading his disciples he is Such.
mn49:30.14: The effluents that defile, that lead to further becoming, that disturb, that ripen in stress, that tend to future birth, aging, & death: Those the Tathāgata has abandoned,
mn49:30.15: their root destroyed, made like a palmyra stump, deprived of the conditions of development, not destined for future arising.
mn49:30.16: Just as a palmyra tree with its crown cut off is incapable of growing again;
mn49:30.17: so, too, the effluents that defile, that lead to further becoming, that disturb, that ripen in stress, that tend to future birth, aging, & death: Those the Tathāgata has abandoned,
mn49:30.18: their root destroyed, made like a palmyra stump, deprived of the conditions of development, not destined for future arising.’”
mn49:31.1: Thus, because of the silencing of Māra, and because of the Brahmā’s invitation, this discourse is entitled,
